arabic_text:
کلمہ
امام
وصی
محتجب
عین عظیمہ
حجابات
عالم جسمانی
علی
حیدر کرار
امیر النحل
یعصب الدین
اباطراب
english_text:
Kalima (Word)
Imam
Wasi
Veiled
Great Eye
Veils
Physical World
Ali
Haydar Karrar
Amir al-Nahl
Ya'sib al-Din
Abu Turab
alt_text: Top half: a flower-like cluster of eight pink circles labeled 'امام' (Imam) and 'وصی' (Wasi) surrounding a central yellow circle labeled 'محتجب' (Veiled) with 'عین عظیمہ' (Great Eye) inside, all under the heading 'کلمہ' (Kalima/Word). Bottom half: a glowing translucent human silhouette labeled 'علی' (Ali) standing in the 'عالم جسمانی' (Physical World), with epithets of Ali listed on the left side (Haydar Karrar, Amir al-Nahl, Ya'sib al-Din, Abu Turab). 'حجابات' (Veils) is labeled on the right between top and bottom halves.
notes: Composite cosmological-soteriological diagram. Upper half depicts the spiritual realm of Kalima with the veiled Great Eye surrounded by Imam/Wasi figures. Lower half projects this into the physical world via Ali (with his classical epithets). The veils (حجابات) separate the spiritual flower from the embodied Ali below — a depiction of theophany through the Imamate.
